blocks.content:
Sometimes a program has values that should never change.

For example:

• the number of days in a week
• the number of months in a year
• the maximum number of players in a game

For such values we use constants.

A constant is a variable whose value cannot be changed after it is created.

Here the keyword `const` is used.

It tells the program that the value of this variable cannot be changed.

For example, the number of days in a week is always `7`.

This value should stay the same in the program.
Retype this code and check it.

Create your own constant.

Name it `monthsInYear` and assign the value `12`.

Type the code yourself using the example above.

The code example below is wrong.

When you use `const`, the value cannot be changed later.

If you try to change it, the program will show an error.
